microsoft_ft: **Owned end-to-end quality program delivery** for Azure's recovery validation portfolio, scaling from a 2-person operation into a platformized system that sustained 45+ annual resilience drills and drove **$14M+ in customer impact** across Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **0→1 build of an internal Resilience Automation Platform**, defining technical requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that reduced operational toil by 30% and enabled a scalable, auditor-style execution model across distributed engineering teams.

Developed **LLM-powered automation** to root-cause drill planning inefficiencies, building an AI agent that cut planning cycle time by 39% and increased quarterly drill capacity by 21% — directly mirroring the JD's call for leveraging LLMs and scripting to auto-evaluate quality.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a rack-level drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a continuous-validation standard adopted across the org.

Directed **14 cross-org program executions** under executive visibility, serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract — reporting quality KPIs and audit results directly to senior leadership.

Maintained and presented **leadership-facing quality performance dashboards**, monitoring SLA adherence and surfacing process improvement opportunities across engineering, account operations, and partner stakeholder teams in high-urgency delivery windows.

Devised auditor performance evaluation frameworks across a distributed specialist workforce, establishing adherence standards and coaching mechanisms that drove measurable quality improvements in human data deliverable accuracy.
microsoft_2023: **Championed adoption of AI-driven workflows** across 14 Azure service teams, conducting user demos and training sessions that drove measurable utilization of LLM-powered code generation tooling and saved **37 engineering hours monthly** in delivery overhead.

Rooted out critical feature gaps in quality and data delivery pipelines by facilitating **11+ structured user interviews** with Azure service teams, directly influencing roadmap prioritization to include intent-based YAML generation as a high-impact automation capability.

Improved data quality and retrieval performance by implementing rigorous metadata standards and migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search platform**, reducing lookup time by 83% and establishing a scalable knowledge-management foundation.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and reduced region launch timelines by 28% by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework across 140+ teams — a scrappy, results-driven program built from scratch under delivery pressure.

Conducted discovery with 20+ service teams to **root-cause 81 hours of manual operational toil** per region launch, then built and socialized a roadmap to automate critical execution paths and eliminate recurring process inefficiencies at scale.

Engineered a **Power BI KPI dashboard** to surface operational toil and automation gaps across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to prioritize high-impact quality improvements and make data-first decisions on program resourcing and SLA management.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by owning the legacy OS migration program end-to-end — mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations and sequencing execution to protect delivery SLAs in a high-urgency environment.

Facilitated Agile sprint planning and retrospectives for a distributed engineering team, **ruthlessly prioritizing 40+ high-priority tickets** to stabilize the developer intake process and maintain program velocity through a complex, multi-team migration.

Drove cross-functional alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and establishing a scalable, process-improved delivery model across the robotics fleet.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by owning the end-to-end operational lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals — redesigning the CRM workflow from scratch to standardize scoping, invoicing, and quality control checkpoints across the full delivery pipeline.

Reduced Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and boosted conversion rates by 2.7% by executing a data-first, digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing web presence and Google profile performance through rigorous qualitative and quantitative analysis.

Conducted financial profitability analysis across **1,000+ project records** to root-cause margin leaks, delivering leadership-facing reports with actionable pricing model improvements — demonstrating the same data-first, results-driven approach the Quality PM role demands.

tailoring_notes: 'Root-cause' (JD: 'root-cause issues') woven into microsoft_ft bullet 2, amazon_robotics bullet 1, and pro_painters bullet 3 to mirror exact JD phrasing.
'LLM-powered automation' and 'leveraging LLMs and scripting' (JD: 'leveraging LLMs, databases, and scripting where applicable') surfaced in microsoft_ft bullet 3 and microsoft_2023 bullet 1.

'Leadership-facing quality performance dashboards' and 'KPI' reporting (JD: 'leadership-facing reports to inform company operations and strategy') placed in microsoft_ft bullet 5 and microsoft_2022 bullet 3.

'Auditor performance evaluation' and 'distributed specialist workforce' (JD: 'evaluate auditor and human data talent performance' / 'hire and maintain a 30+ auditor workforce') introduced in microsoft_ft bullet 7 to directly map Cyrus's team-management scope to Mercor's workforce model.
'Data-first' and 'results-driven' framing (JD: 'data-first approach', 'results-driven leader') used in microsoft_2022 bullet 1 and pro_painters bullet 3 to echo Mercor's stated cultural language.

'SLA adherence' and 'process improvement opportunities' (JD: 'delivering SLAs for high-priority projects' / 'identifying process improvement opportunities') placed in microsoft_ft bullet 6 and amazon_robotics bullet 2.
